mission: Trace the exact flow from user's console logs to identify where coordinates get incorrectly transformed

step_1_php_output.format_detected: Golden Standard format (flat properties: left, top, scaleX, scaleY)

coordinates_extracted.status: CORRECT - These coordinates are ready for 1:1 rendering

step_1_php_output.problem: Metadata is incomplete - missing modern format markers (capture_version, designer_offset)

step_2_frontend_detection.console_log: 🔍 AGENT 1 MUTEX: Format = LEGACY_DB (missing modern metadata)
step_2_frontend_detection.problem: Detection is CORRECT based on metadata, but coordinates are ALREADY in Golden Standard format (no correction needed)

step_3_transformation.console_log_1: 🎯 LEGACY DATA CORRECTION: Legacy data detected - applying data transformation...
step_3_transformation.console_log_2: 🔧 LEGACY DATA CORRECTION: Applying correction matrix: { deltaY: 80, scaleFactor: 1.23, ... }
step_3_transformation.console_log_3: 🔧 LEGACY DATA CORRECTION: Element transformed: { top: '154.555 → 234.555', scaleY: '0.104 → 0.128' }
step_3_transformation.problem: Transformation ASSUMES coordinates are in OLD legacy format, but they're ALREADY CORRECT from PHP conversion!

visual_impact.user_experience: Visual misalignment - logos appear in wrong position

step_4_result.reason: Double transformation - coordinates were already correct but got transformed again

transformation_verification.evidence: Line 6568 in class-octo-print-designer-wc-integration.php shows convert_processed_views_to_canvas_data() extracts flat properties (left, top, scaleX, scaleY) directly from _db_processed_views data which are ALREADY in Golden Standard format

format_contradiction.php_says: Golden Standard format (objects array with flat properties)
format_contradiction.php_evidence: Function detects 'Golden Standard format' and extracts flat properties directly
format_contradiction.actual_format: HYBRID FORMAT - Golden Standard coordinates WITH legacy metadata marker
format_contradiction.contradiction_reason: PHP adds 'source: db_processed_views' to indicate data ORIGIN (where it came from), but frontend interprets this as data FORMAT (needs legacy correction)

console_log_evidence.php_log: Found 2 objects in Golden Standard format
console_log_evidence.contradiction: Both logs are technically correct - objects ARE Golden Standard, but metadata LOOKS LIKE legacy

critical_finding.question: Is Legacy Correction being incorrectly applied?

evidence.description: PHP convert_processed_views_to_canvas_data() detects Golden Standard format
evidence.action: Extracts flat properties: left, top, width, height, scaleX, scaleY, angle
evidence.result: Coordinates are in Golden Standard format (CORRECT for 1:1 rendering)

evidence.description: PHP adds metadata.source = 'db_processed_views' as a MARKER
evidence.purpose: To indicate data was converted from print database (_db_processed_views)
evidence.intended_meaning: Data SOURCE indicator (where data came from)
evidence.side_effect: Frontend interprets this as 'data is in legacy format and needs correction'

evidence.description: Frontend classifyDataFormat() sees marker and triggers legacy detection

evidence.transformation: Adds +80px to top, multiplies scale by 1.23
evidence.problem: These coordinates don't need correction - they're already in Golden Standard format!

critical_finding.root_cause_explanation: The 'db_processed_views' marker was intended to indicate data SOURCE (where it came from), but frontend interprets it as data FORMAT (needs legacy correction). The PHP function ALREADY converts to Golden Standard format, so no frontend correction is needed.
critical_finding.bug_severity: HIGH - Causes visible misalignment in all orders with _db_processed_views data

transformation_1.coordinate_format_input: Either legacy nested format OR Golden Standard flat format
transformation_1.coordinate_format_output: Golden Standard flat format (left, top, scaleX, scaleY)

transformation_2.action: Applies +80px vertical offset and ×1.23 scale correction
transformation_2.result: Coordinates are now WRONG (moved 80px down, scaled 23% larger)
transformation_2.status: ✗ INCORRECT TRANSFORMATION (should not have been applied)

double_transformation_proof.conclusion: Data is being corrected TWICE - once in PHP (correctly) and again in JavaScript (incorrectly). This is a classic double-transformation bug.

provided_logs.interpretation: PHP added this metadata to indicate data was converted from database
provided_logs.problem: Missing capture_version and designer_offset markers that would indicate 'modern' format

provided_logs.log: 🔍 AGENT 1 MUTEX: Format = LEGACY_DB (missing modern metadata)
provided_logs.interpretation: Frontend detects as legacy because metadata is incomplete
provided_logs.problem: Detection logic is TOO SIMPLE - only checks for metadata presence, not actual coordinate format

provided_logs.log: 🔧 LEGACY DATA CORRECTION: Applying correction matrix: { deltaY: 80, scaleFactor: 1.23, source: 'measured_order_5378' }
provided_logs.problem: Coordinates don't need fixing - they're already correct from PHP conversion!

provided_logs.log: 🔧 LEGACY DATA CORRECTION: Element transformed: { top: '154.555 → 234.555', scaleY: '0.104 → 0.128' }
provided_logs.problem: Success message is misleading - data is now WRONG, not fixed

contradiction_in_logs.php_log: Found 2 objects in Golden Standard format
contradiction_in_logs.truth: Objects ARE in Golden Standard format, but metadata LOOKS LIKE legacy format
contradiction_in_logs.why_both_are_correct: PHP log refers to COORDINATE FORMAT (Golden Standard). Frontend log refers to METADATA FORMAT (legacy-style incomplete metadata).

option_1.name: Add modern metadata markers in PHP (RECOMMENDED)
option_1.code_after:
'metadata' => [
  'source' => 'db_processed_views',
  'capture_version' => '2.1-converted',  // NEW: Marks as modern format
  'designer_offset' => [                  // NEW: Indicates no offset needed
    'x' => 0,
    'y' => 0
  ],
  'converted_at' => current_time('mysql'),
  'order_id' => $order_id,
  'template_id' => $template_id,
  'original_view_name' => $first_view['view_name'] ?? 'Design View',
  'mockup_source' => $mockup_url ? 'template/product' : 'none'
]

impact.coordinate_integrity: Coordinates will remain CORRECT (no transformation applied)
impact.compatibility: Backward compatible - existing modern data unaffected

pros: No changes needed to frontend detection logic
option_1.recommendation: STRONGLY RECOMMENDED - This is the cleanest and most maintainable solution

option_2.name: Change metadata source value

impact.legacy_correction: Will fall through to Method 2 (missing metadata check)
impact.risk: May still be detected as legacy if capture_version/designer_offset missing

cons: May still trigger legacy detection via Method 2
option_2.recommendation: ACCEPTABLE but not as robust as Option 1

option_3.name: Improve frontend detection logic
option_3.change_description: Add coordinate format inspection to classifyDataFormat()

impact.legacy_correction: Won't be triggered for Golden Standard coordinates
impact.maintenance: More complex detection logic to maintain

option_3.recommendation: GOOD FOR LONG-TERM but higher implementation cost

rationale: Minimal code changes required (add 2 properties to PHP metadata)
No changes needed to frontend logic
Clear semantic meaning (capture_version indicates modern format)
testing_requirements: Verify no regression in existing functionality

summary.root_cause: Double transformation bug - data converted to Golden Standard in PHP, then incorrectly transformed again in JavaScript due to metadata marker confusion
summary.impact: All orders with _db_processed_views data render with 80px vertical offset and 23% scale error
summary.fix: Add capture_version and designer_offset to metadata in PHP convert_processed_views_to_canvas_data() function
summary.confidence: 100% - Root cause confirmed through code trace and console log analysis
